SECURITY.AUTHENTICATION_TYPE: Session-based UUID (client-generated and persistent in localStorage, or optionally mapped to Supabase Auth user accounts)

ENDPOINT.DESCRIPTION: Accepts a user mood and optional open-ended journal text. If text is provided, parses it using a Groq completion query to extract: 1. The dominant emotion (e.g. stress, anxiety, happiness, calm). 2. A list of specific exam-prep triggers (e.g. mock test, time limit, physics prep, math pressure). Persists the final analysis into the `daily_logs` database and returns the generated metadata.

RESPONSE.DESCRIPTION: Log successfully processed, parsed by LLM, and saved to the database.

ERROR.REASON: Missing or invalid request properties (e.g. empty fields, invalid UUID).
LLM API failure, Supabase database offline, or parsing error.

ENDPOINT.DESCRIPTION: Fetches the student's log history and analytics metrics. Computes 7-day mood history and stress trigger tag frequencies for the student dashboard.

PARAMETER.name: user_id
PARAMETER.description: Unique UUID identifying the student session

PARAMETER.name: days
PARAMETER.description: Number of days history to retrieve

RESPONSE.DESCRIPTION: Success. Returns matching historical records and aggregated dashboard metadata.

ENDPOINT.DESCRIPTION: Handles dialogue messages with the empathetic chat companion. 1. SAFETY PRE-CHECK: Parses user input for critical keywords denoting self-harm, suicide, or crisis. - If a keyword matches: Immediately stops, bypasses Groq API, inserts a crisis marker entry in the chat database, and returns flagged=true with crisis helpline numbers. 2. CLEAN PROMPT PATH: If input is safe, queries Groq completions API with: - A structured system prompt (grounded in clinical wellness frameworks, limits discussions to competitive exam stress). - A negative prompt (forbids solving homework, coding, or answering general subjects). - Previous session chat history. Saves the final query & response pair in `chat_history`.

RESPONSE.DESCRIPTION: Success. Response may be flagged (safety bypass triggered) or unflagged (normal conversation path).

ERROR.REASON: Missing or invalid request payload parameters.

POLICY.name: Crisis Interception
POLICY.BEHAVIOR: If the incoming student chat message matches any of the listed substrings (case-insensitive, using boundary match or word root scanning): 1. Halt processing immediately. 2. DO NOT make a network call to the Groq API. 3. Insert the user's message and the standard crisis notification message into the `chat_history` database table. 4. Return the flagged JSON response payload (status 200, success=true, flagged=true, emergency helplines payload). 5. Frontend must capture `flagged: true` and immediately render the styled red card on screen, hiding standard AI interaction components if necessary.

LLM_PROMPT_GUARDS.GUARD: You are an empathetic, supportive mental wellness assistant tailored for students preparing for high-stakes examinations (such as JEE, NEET, board exams, UPSC, and CUET). Your sole mission is to offer stress management advice, emotional grounding, time-management tips, anxiety control strategies, and reassurance. You must ground your advice in clinical wellness frameworks (such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy techniques, progressive muscle relaxation, mindfulness, and breathing patterns). Always provide short, warm, and highly structured suggestions. Cite guidelines like the World Health Organization (WHO) or Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW) for stress-management techniques where appropriate.

You are strictly forbidden from helping the student with homework, academic curriculum, coding questions, maths equations, or scientific problem-solving. If the user asks questions such as "How do I solve this physics problem?", "Explain the citric acid cycle", or "Write a Python script", you must politely decline and remind them that your role is solely to support their emotional and mental well-being during exam preparation. Never prescribe or suggest specific medical drugs (e.g. anti-depressants, sleeping pills) - instruct them to seek medical professionals.
